next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 3

For this, she'd overprepared. Of course she had. Three parchments of diagnostic protocols lay beside two standard Dark-object assessment frameworks and a sequence of probing charms she'd modified to account for the layered binding described in Baruffio's monograph on voluntary curse-binding. The literature assumed a compliant subject, adequate workspace, controlled light. It did not assume sustained contact with Draco Malfoy.

Across from her, at the oak table in the study adjoining the library, he sat with his left sleeve rolled to the elbow and his forearm laid flat between them like evidence entered into a file.

Morning light made the room usable. That was why she'd chosen it: east-facing windows, a sturdy table, neutral ground between his rooms and the library she'd claimed as workspace. Notes, wand, and annotation quill waited in strict order.

"I'll need to touch your arm," she said. "Sustained contact. Some of the probing charms need a physical anchor."

"I assumed."

"I'm telling you so you're not startled."

"I won't be startled." His gaze stayed on the far wall.

A chair leg scraped when she moved closer. From this distance the Mark looked nothing like the wartime photographs or Ministry records. In those images it had been vivid, almost aggressive: skull, serpent, ink-black lines that moved as if they had appetite. On his forearm it had faded toward dark grey. Not gone. Nothing about a Dark Lord's brand simply went away. But the black had softened, the edges had blurred, the serpent's motion had slowed to a tired twitch. It looked like a bruise that...
open_hooks_to_carry: Draco's isolation rendered concretely — the Manor too large, his life too small, careful and precise and starved
Hermione arrives; the negotiation is exact, professional, last-name-basis
The Dark Mark's new behavior — pain without signal, magic restless
The Manor opens a forgotten corridor when she enters — Draco registers this silently
prev_tail_excerpt:
...heroism, not wisdom, just the worn-down expression of those who still hadn't worked out what the surviving was going to cost.

She rolled onto her side, battered the pillow flatter, closed her eyes, and got the stabilization index first. The decay curve followed, numbers moving in the wrong direction. Then came the drawing room: the chandelier, the marble, the sound she had made there.

Her eyes opened again.

Children were starting to misfire all over the country. She'd read the reports herself: accidental magic spiking in Muggle-born homes, windows blown out, kitchens scorched, parents with no language for what was happening to their children. In the Atrium that same week a maintenance wizard had fought a bucket because the fountain could no longer keep water in it. Britain was fraying in small failures. Hermione knew more about that fraying than anyone else alive.

Fear did not become irrational just because it interfered with useful work. The body kept its own records. Under the glamour the scar tissue still rose from her left forearm in hard ridges, letters carved in that house by that hand. Soon she would walk back into the place that had made them.

Sleep finally took her sometime after three, and even that felt closer to collapse than rest. At six the alarm went off to a cold room: the warming charm had bled out during the night. Then came the old sequence of repair. She showered, faced the mirror, cast the glamour, pulled the sleeve down, buttoned the cuff. By the time she left for work, concealment was smooth again and the scar was hidden where it had always been.
secondary_character_beats: Narcissa referenced in passing — her absence from the Manor noted as recent, deliberate

setups_to_plant.description: The house opens a corridor Draco forgot existed the moment Hermione enters — first sign of differential treatment
The drawing room is not mentioned but Draco's awareness of specific rooms — which ones he avoids, which corridors he doesn't take — establishes that the Manor's geography is emotionally loaded

state_deltas_required: Two people with brutal shared history must negotiate terms of sustained proximity; the Manor is already responding to her presence
Terms set — they are bound to proximity. The house has already started changing, and Draco notices but doesn't mention it
